---
title: "ledoit_wolf"
generated: true
description: "Ledoit-Wolf analytic-shrinkage covariance estimator."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.connectivity`](../nitrix-stats-connectivity.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:ledoit_wolf -->
```python
ledoit_wolf(X: Float[Array, 'n p'], *, assume_centered: bool = False) -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'p p'], Float[Array, '']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:ledoit_wolf -->
Ledoit-Wolf analytic-shrinkage covariance estimator.

Forms the convex blend $(1 - \alpha) S + \alpha \mu I$ of the
sample covariance $S$ toward the scaled identity
$\mu I$ (with $\mu = \operatorname{tr}(S) / p$), using the
closed-form Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage intensity

$$
\alpha = \beta^2 / \delta^2,
\qquad \delta^2 = \lVert S - \mu I \rVert_F^2,
\qquad \beta^2 = \frac{1}{n^2} \sum_k \lVert x_k \rVert^4
- \frac{1}{n} \lVert S \rVert_F^2,
$$

with $\beta^2$ clipped to $[0, \delta^2]$.  This matches
`sklearn.covariance.ledoit_wolf`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:ledoit_wolf -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `X` | `Float` | `n` × `p` | — | Data matrix of `n` observations by `p` features. |
| `assume_centered` | `bool` | — | `False` | If `True`, treat `X` as already mean-centred; otherwise centre each feature before estimating the covariance. Default `False`. |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'p p'], Float[Array, '']]`. The shrunk covariance matrix $\hat{\Sigma}$. The scalar shrinkage intensity $\alpha \in [0, 1]$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:ledoit_wolf -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
